title: F041 — Unity Without Collapse

requiredObjects: central registry or shared protocol layer
central symbolic heart or integration point

composition.colorLogic: cool scientific background, bright path lines, restrained accent colors
composition.labelPolicy: minimal labels, no dense paragraphs inside image

qaChecklist: Avoids forbidden objects and fake text
Does not overuse mystical or decorative elements

prompts.providerAgnostic:
Create a publication-quality scientific book figure titled "F041 — Unity Without Collapse".
Purpose: Distinct centers remain themselves while participating in shared coherence.

Manuscript context: Examples include: itemize item justice and mercy, item death and resurrection, item self and other, item matter and spirit, item time and eternity, item individuality and communion. itemize F041 — Unity Without CollapseDistinct centers remain themselves while participating in shared coherence.F041-unity-without-collapse Scientific Integrity Note This chapter does not claim that Christic equivalency is a physical law. It does not claim that theology proves quantum mechanics or that quantum mechanics proves Christology. It claims that equivalency is a powerful symbolic operator for the book's metaphysical architecture. The scientific language of the wider manuscript must remain carefully distinguished from this theological-symbolic center. FHQCM Interpretation Christic Harmonic Equivalency is the model's reconciliation principle. It says that separation is real at one scale but not ultimate. It says distinction matters, but isolation is not final. It says the deepest coherence does not destroy persons, histories, wounds, bodies, or worlds. It transfigures them into communion. In the language of recovery, it is the movement from fragmentation to integration. In the language of cosmology, it is the return of multiplicity into a higher-order unity. In the language of faith, it is resurrection.

Scientific/conceptual concepts: wave-particle relationship, quantum state transition, federated systems architecture, symbolic integration.
Required visual objects: wavefront or probability field, measurement boundary, nodes, links, central registry or shared protocol layer, central symbolic heart or integration point, spiral or healing path.
Recommended short labels: superposition, measurement, node, federation, integration, recovery.

Composition: left-to-right experimental flow.
Camera/view: orthographic.
Visual hierarchy: source/emission → path or choice point → measurement/detector → observer/information interpretation.
Color logic: cool scientific background, bright path lines, restrained accent colors.
Label policy: minimal labels, no dense paragraphs inside image.

Avoid: fake citations, fake equations, unreadable text, watermarks, copyrighted characters, random mystical clutter, misleading scientific labels, random galaxies unless explicitly required, decorative clutter.

Make it clean, serious, elegant, uncluttered, and suitable for an academic manuscript.
